name: PNG Data Points Analysis
description: Game performance analysis from PNG filename metadata. Extracts triangle counts, draw calls, and timestamps from filename patterns.
author: BobReview

labels._comment: Labels are optional - templates have inline defaults. Only define overrides here.
labels.footer_text: Generated by BobReview - Performance Analysis and Review Tool

llm_generators.name: Executive Summary
llm_generators.description: High-level performance overview and key findings

categories.title: Overall Performance Health
categories.focus: reference P90/P95 values for realistic expectations

categories.title: Key Concerns
categories.focus: based on trends and variability (CV indicates consistency)

categories.title: Peak Hotspot Impact
categories.focus: mention the peak hotspot and its severity

llm_generators.prompt_template: Analyze draw call and triangle performance data.

categories.title: Performance Consistency
categories.focus: based on CV and confidence intervals

categories.title: Performance Trajectory
categories.focus: whether action is needed based on trends

categories.title: LOD System
categories.focus: prioritize if triangles trend is degrading or CV is high

categories.title: Occlusion and Visibility
categories.focus: prioritize if draw calls are high

categories.title: Lighting and Shadows
categories.focus: shadow quality, dynamic vs baked lighting

categories.title: Materials and Textures
categories.focus: prioritize if draw call CV is high

categories.title: Capture and Regression
categories.focus: emphasize trend monitoring if trends are degrading

pages.card_description: Detailed statistical analysis of draw calls and triangle counts with interactive timelines and correlation charts.
Identify high-load and low-load frames, critical hotspots, and performance zones requiring optimization.
Distribution timelines and visual breakdowns showing performance patterns across all captures.
Actionable recommendations for addressing critical hotspots and high-load frames with budget guidelines.
Comprehensive statistical analysis including percentiles, confidence intervals, and outlier detection.

content_blocks.performance_budgets_intro: Establish and maintain performance budgets to ensure consistent frame rates and responsive gameplay. Any capture exceeding the hard cap should be treated as a performance issue.

budget_guidelines.title: Soft Cap
budget_guidelines.description: Target performance level for most scenes. Aim to stay below this threshold.

budget_guidelines.title: Hard Cap
budget_guidelines.description: Maximum acceptable performance cost. Exceeding this requires immediate optimization.

budget_guidelines.title: Budget Tracking
budget_guidelines.description: Monitor budgets across all development stages to prevent performance regressions.

content_blocks.critical_hotspot_intro: The highest-priority optimization target. This frame has the worst performance characteristics and should be addressed first for maximum impact.
critical_hotspot_actions: Profile this specific frame to identify expensive draw calls
Look for redundant or unnecessary rendering operations
Consider LOD (Level of Detail) systems for distant objects
Investigate potential for object culling or occlusion
Batch similar materials and meshes where possible

geometry_strategies.title: LOD Systems
geometry_strategies.description: Implement multiple levels of detail for complex meshes

geometry_strategies.title: Mesh Simplification
geometry_strategies.description: Reduce polygon counts on distant or less important objects

geometry_strategies.title: Frustum Culling
geometry_strategies.description: Ensure objects outside the camera view are not rendered

geometry_strategies.title: Occlusion Culling
geometry_strategies.description: Skip rendering objects hidden behind other geometry

geometry_strategies.title: Instancing
geometry_strategies.description: Use GPU instancing for repeated geometry

draw_call_strategies.title: Batching
draw_call_strategies.description: Combine meshes with identical materials into single draw calls

draw_call_strategies.title: Material Reduction
draw_call_strategies.description: Minimize unique material instances and shader variants

draw_call_strategies.title: Atlasing
draw_call_strategies.description: Combine multiple textures into texture atlases

draw_call_strategies.title: GPU Instancing
draw_call_strategies.description: Render multiple copies of the same mesh in one call

draw_call_strategies.title: Static Batching
draw_call_strategies.description: Pre-combine static geometry at build time

draw_call_strategies.title: Dynamic Batching
draw_call_strategies.description: Combine small dynamic objects at runtime

content_blocks.system_recommendations_intro: Strategic recommendations for improving overall performance architecture and workflow.
content_blocks.visual_analysis_intro: Distribution charts reveal patterns in performance data, showing how metrics are distributed across all captures. This helps identify clustering, outliers, and overall performance characteristics.
content_blocks.priority_matrix_intro: Prioritize optimization work based on impact and effort. Focus on high-impact, low-effort improvements first.
content_blocks.zones_overview_intro: Performance zones categorize captures based on their resource usage. This helps identify problematic areas requiring optimization and establish baseline performance expectations.
content_blocks.critical_hotspot_zones: The single worst-performing capture that demands the highest priority for optimization work.
content_blocks.critical_hotspot_analysis: This frame represents the peak load in the capture session. Focus optimization efforts here first, as improvements will have the most significant impact.
content_blocks.high_load_description: High-load is defined as Draw Calls ≥ {high_load_draw_threshold} or Triangles ≥ {high_load_tri_threshold}. These frames require immediate attention and optimization.
content_blocks.low_load_description: Low-load frames (Draw Calls < {low_load_draw_threshold} and Triangles < {low_load_tri_threshold}) represent baseline performance and are useful design references for optimal scenes.
